briefdescription.para: Timeout count for CSI soft reset.
Stub callback function for asynchronous error interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: This routine is a stub for the asynchronous error interrupt callback.

Each of callback functions to be called on different types of interrupts. These stub functions are set during XCsi_CfgInitialize as default callback functions. If application is not registered any of the callback function, these functions will be called for doing nothing.
The stub is here in case the upper layer forgot to set the handler. On initialization, Error interrupt handler is set to this callback. It is considered an error for this handler to be invoked.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the error. Its value equals 'OR'ing one or more XCSI_ISR_*_MASK values defined in xcsi_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: Initialize the XCsi instance provided by the caller based on the given Config structure.

detaileddescription.para: Initialize the CSI driver.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
CfgPtr
is the device configuration structure containing information about a specific CSI.
EffectiveAddr
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.
XST_SUCCESS if initialization was successful.
None. This function initializes the CSI driver/device. This function must be called prior to using the driver. Initialization includes setting up the instance data and ensuring the hardware is in a quiescent state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is a reference to a structure containing information about a specific XCsi instance.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

briefdescription.para: This function will do a reset of the IP.

detaileddescription.para: Reset the CSI controller.
This will reset the values of all registers except Core Config and Protocol Config registers.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
XST_SUCCESS on proper reset.
XST_FAILURE on timeout and core being stuck in reset.
None. This function resets the CSI controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.

briefdescription.para: This function will enable/disable the IP Core to start processing.

detaileddescription.para: Activate or deactivate the CSI controller.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
Flag
will be used to indicate Enable or Disable action.
XST_SUCCESS on successful core enable or disable.
XST_FAILURE if core disable times out.
None. This function activates or deactivates the CSI controller based on the Flag parameter.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is the enable/disable flag. Set to 1 to enable, 0 to disable.

briefdescription.para: This function will configure the core with proper number of Active Lanes.

detaileddescription.para: Configure the CSI controller.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
XST_SUCCESS on configuring the core.
XST_FAILURE if active lanes not set correctly.
None. This function configures the CSI controller with the active lanes setting.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.

briefdescription.para: This function will control the virtual channels selection dynamically.

detaileddescription.para: Set the virtual channel selection.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
Value
will set the virtual channels corresponding to each bit value. bit value 1: core processes packets with this VC, bit value 0: core filters packets with this VC.
None.
None. This function sets the virtual channel selection for filtering.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is the virtual channel selection value to be set.

briefdescription.para: This function will return the virtual channels selected.

detaileddescription.para: Get the virtual channel selection.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
Value of selected VCs.
None. This function returns the current virtual channel selection.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.

briefdescription.para: This function will get the short packet received in the FIFO from the Generic Short Packet Register and fill up the structure passed from caller.

detaileddescription.para: Get short packet information.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
ShortPacketStruct
is going to be filled up by this function and returned to the caller.
None.
None. This function retrieves the short packet information from the controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is a pointer to the structure where short packet data will be stored.

briefdescription.para: This function will get the information about the state of the Clock Lane.

detaileddescription.para: Get clock lane information.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
ClkLane
is going to be filled up by this function and returned to the caller.
None.
None. This function retrieves the clock lane information from the controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is a pointer to the structure where clock lane info will be stored.

briefdescription.para: This function will get the information about the state of a Data Lane.

detaileddescription.para: Get data lane information.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
Lane
is the Lane number whose information is requested.
DataLane
is going to be filled up by this function and returned to the caller.
None.
None. This function retrieves the data lane information for a specific lane.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is the lane number (0 to 3) for which to get information.
is a pointer to the structure where data lane info will be stored.

briefdescription.para: This function will get the line count, byte count and data type information about a Virtual Channel.

detaileddescription.para: Get virtual channel information.

InstancePtr
is the XCsi instance to operate on.
Vc
is the Virtual Channel number whose information is requested.
VCInfo
is going to be filled up by this function and returned to the caller.
None.
None. This function retrieves the virtual channel information.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is the virtual channel number for which to get information.
is a pointer to the structure where virtual channel info will be stored.

briefdescription.para: This function checks the validity of the active lanes parameter.

detaileddescription.para: Check if active lane count is valid.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Subsystem instance to be worked on.
ActiveLanesCount
is the lane count to check if valid.
1 if specified Active Lanes is valid.
0 otherwise, if the Active Lanes specified isn't valid as per spec and design.
None. This function validates if the specified active lane count is valid for the current configuration.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCsi instance.
is the active lane count to be validated.

listitem.para: 1 if the active lane count is valid.
0 if the active lane count is invalid.
